name: Clive Younghusband's Venison Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Red or white wine vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour half a bottle of red wine and half the wine vinegar into a non-aluminum pan. Add bay leaves, allspice, and vegetables. Do not season. Bring to a boil for 30 minutes. Cool to room temperature. This can be done overnight in the refrigerator but allow it to return to room temperature before adding meat. Strain the mixture through cheesecloth. Discard the vegetables and spices in the cheesecloth.
2. Add 2 tablespoons sugar and 2 tablespoons salt to the liquid. Cut venison into 2-inch cubes. Add to the strained mixture. Let the mixture stand in the refrigerator for 24 hours.
3. Sauté mushrooms and onions until limp. Add gravy, garlic, oregano, and the remaining red wine.
4. Fry salt pork until crisp. Drain. Add salt pork to the mushroom/gravy mixture.
5. Remove venison from marinade. Throw away the marinade.
6. Sauté venison until brown. Add to mushroom/gravy mixture. Place the mixture in an oven-proof casserole and cover. Bake at 350°F for 2 hours or until venison is done.
7. Serve stew over wild rice with cranberry sauce on the side.
